prompt: Do not use <think> tags.
Do not reason before generating.
Your response must begin with <nn> immediately.
Your first output character must be '<'.
Do not write any text before <nn>.
Do not write any text after </tr>.
Output only these three XML blocks in order: <nn>...</nn><hp>...</hp><tr>...</tr>.
You are generating a LEMUR-compatible neural network for dataset='{dataset}', task='{task}', metric='{metric}', epoch={epoch}.
Target accuracy above {acc_2}.
References A and B are architecturally very different — synthesize a new architecture combining their best ideas.
Regardless of the reference style, always follow this exact code structure:
1. Standard imports at top level (torch, torchvision, etc.)
2. TorchVision helper class
3. adaptive_pool_flatten function
4. supported_hyperparameters() at module level
5. class Net(nn.Module) containing only: __init__, infer_dimensions_dynamically, forward, train_setup, learn
Never place train_setup or learn outside the Net class.
Never use relative imports like 'from .module import X' — use absolute imports only.
self.device = device must be set in Net.__init__.
Use at most two backbone models total — never three.
Only use these backbone models: 'resnet50', 'densenet169', 'mobilenet_v3_large', 'efficientnet_b0', 'efficientnet_b3'.
Always use weights='DEFAULT' when instantiating TorchVision.
supported_hyperparameters() must return exactly: 'batch', 'dropout', 'epoch', 'lr', 'momentum', 'transform'.
Every key in supported_hyperparameters() must appear at least twice in the code by exact name.
The <hp> block must contain a valid JSON dict with exactly these keys: batch, dropout, epoch, lr, momentum, transform.
Never generate a list in <hp> — only a JSON object.
The 'transform' value in <hp> must be exactly 'norm_256_flip'.
Net.__init__ signature must be: def __init__(self, in_shape, out_shape, prm, device).
self.features() must receive raw input x — never backbone output.

<hp>{hp_1}</hp>
<tr>{tr_1}</tr>
<nn>{nn_1}</nn>
